reviews.review_description:
It actually pains me a little to give this app four stars instead of five, because in terms of mechanics, it’s my favorite fitness app. But I really miss the systematic approach — a ready-made workout program — where I can just open the app and jump right into my workout. Instead, every time I open it, I have to think and plan which specific tracks and settings to use, which is frustrating and inefficient. Playlists partially solve this problem, but they’re still far from a consistent and continuous workout program. So, even though X-Fitness is my favorite in terms of mechanics, I’m forced to spend more time on the more systematic Les Mills BC. But I’d be happy to switch over to X-Fitness as much as possible if it ever gets a consistent workout program. 
To start with, please add at least a randomizer so that users can play a sequence of random tracks — this probably won’t be very expensive from a development standpoint, but it will greatly improve the usability of the app. 
And the second thing, which other players have already mentioned (and I agree with them) — please, please add more game content (tracks).

I really really like this game. I like the music and the environments. But it doesn't stay at my set height (60 inches.) So I end up boxing above my head. And I'm not sure if it's me or my controllers, or the game itself,  but my punches tend to go right thru the targets. Frustrating to say the least. Please update and fix this! I'd love to revise this review with 5 stars!

This is an excellent game. I’ve only done the trial. I have several year-long subscriptions that after the first of the trial is over, they charge you for the entire year.  I am glad that this is a one time payment and has such diversity.  

In a few weeks, I’ll be 63. I’m getting back into the habit of doing something to encourage my body to burn more energy and lose a little bit of weight as well as get my body back in shape.  (It’s been a long time since I exercised.!)

It makes you think this one quick decisions. I may have to reset and calibrate my room as sometimes the complete scene disappears and I’m getting only half of a room so I don’t see the whole arena.

I would like to see an update so instead of single player, you can have up to seven or eight players all in one area competing for high scores as an alternative.  


Thank you for the opportunity to play the games that you’ve created and hoping for updates and new scenarios and new music challenges and I really like the idea of having up to 4 profiles that is beautiful too!

By the way, thank you for also introducing me to racket pinball. It is absolutely fantastic as well having so much fun in there have never played
Anything like it before!

🤓

It's not bad so far. I've only been using it a week or so. Got some major selling points; one price (I hate subscriptions), and you can use your own MP3s. I'm still working on some of the modifications,  though. I'd like to be able to choose not to have the 'slicers', or at least have a minimal amount of them. I find they break up the rhythm (for me, anyway). I saw something about being able to choose just boxing with my own MP3s, but I haven't found how to do that yet. Again, I'm still new at this one.  Gotta give it 5 stars for the effort. Hope it sees updates every once in a while.

You can't add your own songs. When trying to add/play custom mp3 songs it shows "Access denied grant permission", even tho the App has all the permissions.
Upon searching online, i've come to found out i'm not the only one with said problem

Edit: Found the problem. 1st: "Access denied grant permission" is by default showing, you see that all always, it goes away only when you press on some .mp3 file. That was a bit misleading. 2nd: your files HAVE to be real .mp3 files, no other format, no changing the file extension.

I'm a 67 soon to be 68 disabled woman and I just love this app. Getting better and better on it every day I love the beats music arenas and I do it 15 mins a day im getting myself fit for a spinal operation later this year I want to be strong so I can recover quicker.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sharing your review.  Wishing you all the best. And if you’d like, you’re very welcome to join our community🎮💥

reviews.review_title: Who is giving 1 star reviews to this!
reviews.review_description:
My go to game for aerobic exercise. I get to pick and choose the types of obstacles tempo and use my own mp3's. Bought it on steam and Quest. I use it to this day. Sucker Punch is another great boxing game that did not get any recognition. Anyway, this is one of the pioneer vr games and I enjoy it more than beat sabre. And I love Star Wars! 

Ps The new racket pinball game is darn good as well.  Keep up the good work. Once headset size shrinks or people can steam games to AR glasses you will be ahead of the pack in design.

developer_response.body: We’re happy you enjoy the flexibility (custom MP3s, tempo, obstacles) and that you’re also having fun with Racket Pinball! 🎮💥Feel free to join our community to share suggestions and stay in the loop! 🎶🧠💥

reviews.review_title: Decent Boxing…but get it on sale
reviews.review_description:
Update as of April 2026.  Now giving this app 3.5 stars because there isn’t enough content here and there have been no recent updates.  Plus there is next to no customer support on their facebook page and no available Discord group.  No one will answer your questions when there is a problem.  
This app works well most of the time and the mechanics for boxing are good.  However, there are only 30 songs, and most are only 2 minutes long.  A couple are only a minute long and there is just one 6 minute song/workout.   That’s it.  You can easily go through all the workouts in 2-3 days.  I hardly ever go to this app anymore and that’s a shame because it’s fairly good at the boxing.   

If you don’t care for the dark gym environment of Les Mills, the atmosphere here is definitely brighter and the music is not as irritating.  However it would be appreciated if they added knee strikes for more lower body movement which is lacking.   Also there isn’t a way to adjust the height of the squat bars.  If you make some improvements, offered multiplayer and added about 5 *long* songs, this app could be closer to 4.5 stars.  
—————
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️.   4 stars.
What I like: This app will get you moving and improve your reflexes.  It’s great that I can turn off certain features…like the skulls and the drones, so it is adaptable. The mechanics of the game seem to work well except that sometimes the slicing discs don’t register hits.   I also like the level of speed which allows for good 'form'.  I am playing mostly in the middle range.  The music is decent…non irritating - which I appreciate since it is electronic beat music, but the songs are way too short.

What I would like to see: something that will encourage more leg movements from side to side.   Squats are fine, but sometimes too low.    
Also, to make it more dynamic, you might consider offering one or two moving stage/floor environments (like in SynthRider) as that makes it more fun and visually interesting.  A tutorial for how to bring songs over would be appreciated.  Definite room for improvement.

reviews.review_title: Who's giving positive reviews to this?!
reviews.review_description:
Another "Fitness app" with workout lasting less than 2 Min... Total length of the "workout" in the entire app is like 20 min. Not a fitness app, not a game. Just another power beat VR clone, good for nothing. Copium...
Meta should remove things like this from the store.

I don’t know why people complain. Best part is chose what workouts.  I was in a bad car accident so the apps with 20 min workouts won’t work.  I like to choose what one I’m using and it keeps me moving. I do recommend it to everyone!

Question. I recently injured my left arm.  Just what I needed. Is it possible to add one handed option so can still workout for next few weeks that I’m injured? Thank you!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this review! 🙌 Feel free to join our community to share suggestions and stay in the loop! 🎶🧠💥

reviews.review_title: Great workout but can be improved
reviews.review_description:
Bought this game as a bundle with Racket pinball. I’m  super impressed with both games and am enjoying them! 

However, only thing that annoys me is that both games cannot orient my position correctly on loading, and there is no way in game to adjust it. Every time I define my play space the game puts me in a very odd position and angle within it. It takes several quits and restarts before the app figures out where I am properly in my play space and it’s the only reason why I’m docking a star. 

Otherwise I’m having fun and getting a great workout!
Hi my name is Ashley. When I’m in my wheelchair, I can hit the targets but when I stand up and try, the targets r way to high.

waist of money! they either to tall or too low down, no matter how tall or short i make my height
 on it.

i have problem adding downloaded mp3. i gave and checked all permissions. from main device owner account it works but when i switch to second account Download and Music folders not accessible. in file manager shows storage path as "emulated/10" but app using "emulated/0" in all accounts

Never thought I'd fall in love with another workout app so quickly after FitXR, but here we are. While there's still a lot I don't understand in terms of customization options, how to track my progress (mainly so I can brag with posts like "X-Fitness: 100 weeks straight!" lol, etc., I don't mind because I'd rather jump right in and get moving any way. The ability to workout to your own music is an absolute game changer. Well done peoples. Well done!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot! 😊 Awesome to hear you’re loving X-Fitness! 🎵💪
Feel free to join our community! 😄🔥

reviews.review_title: Great for using your own music
reviews.review_description: I have tried several rhythmic boxing apps, and this one is the best so far. I only use my own music, the app composes maps automatically, and sometimes quite interesting combinations appear. The obstacles are fairly versatile (although I feel that something to make me jump is missing - either a low rope or laser, or a high target that I should hit with my head). The existing backgrounds are enough by now, and in future I would not mind paying for a fresh background pack (hopefully featuring some new types, apart from the very closed and very open ones that are used now).

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review! 😊 We're glad you're enjoying the game. Feel free to join our community to share suggestions and stay in the loop! 🎶🧠💥

reviews.review_title: Seems cool but can't use my own music fo
reviews.review_description: Hi I like the game it works well but for some reason whenever I try to use my own music it Says access denied grant permissions but I've already tripped checked that all the permissions are already turned on but maybe I'm just missing something because I do have 2 profiles on my meta quest 3s but i also made sure all permissions were turned on on that profile too so I have no idea what's going on but like I said I do enjoy it so I'll definitely change to a 5 Star if I can fix that

I work out three days a week with weights but couldn't bring myself to do anything for my endurance. I hated it until I found X fitness, now I can't wait to start my workout. For me, it's by far the best VR game for exactly that. I've tried TotF (not for me), Dance Floor (also not for me), Star Wave (nope I got sick), Synth Rider (which is fun but not overly challenging) and Funfitland (which I enjoy too but you have less options on changing the obstacles) but this one keeps me going. You can spice things up (or down) by adding or excluding obstacles, the objects are on the beat, the controller vibration is on point and overall it's freaking amazing. You really need to push/swing your arms (my PB was on my right arm 19km/h so far but I am working on it). You can also -as I mentioned above- exclude horizontal obstacles in case your knees are hurting or you don't want to squat deep for health reasons. So far I didn't find out how to create a playlist or simply play it for 30min without starting a new song. Maybe if someone reads it and can help me, I appreciate it. Anyways if I had to choose only one game for fitness, it would 1000% this Suggestion : It would be fun if there were obstacles you need to slightly jump over (just for fun not any tracking) or to avoid holes on the ground. I found a lot of games miss this opportunity. Even without tracking, I think it could be fun.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the awesome review! To create your own playlist, click on PLAYLIST with the pointer, then select one of the empty playlists – Alpha, Beta, or the ones below. After selecting the playlist, make sure to press EDIT. Next, choose a song and press ADD. You can add pre-made tracks or your own MP3 files. Once you have selected your songs, press SAVE on the left side. Then, go back to PLAYLIST, select the one you just created, and click PLAY.
We’d love for you to join our community! You’ll find tips, support, and even a video tutorial on how to set up your playlist. Looking forward to seeing you there! 💪

reviews.review_title: Bought before ended the free trial!
reviews.review_description: Update : Thanks to the developers for informing me where I can find kilos and cms! 😁 Additionally I now know my height and weight in inches and pounds....anyway,  it's excellent. Really loving this app. It's challenging enough and very enjoyable ! 5 stars!! I was using another one but it was limited in both workouts and music. I highly recommend this one. One thing I discovered which is interesting. I'm strongly left-handed, but for some reason, I'm finding right hand option is better in VR. And on X-Fitness I have discovered that my right arm and hand is faster, far more accurate and higher stats in every area than my left. That's really surprising and I wonder if any other left handers are right handed in VR ?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review! 😊 Yes, you can switch to metric units. Just go to PROFILES in the Main Menu - when the 4 profiles appear, look in the top right corner and you'll see the option to change UNITS (kg/lbs, cm/inch)

Feel free to join our community to connect with other players and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮🌍

reviews.review_title: Most intense workout among rhythm games
reviews.review_description:
I started playing X-Fitness three months ago, and out of all my rhythm games, it gives me the most intense workout by far, even though I've been playing Synth Riders (in Rhythm mode) and Audio Trip for over two years on their highest difficulty levels, and Beat Saber on Expert.

Not, however, if you only make "lukewarm" punches. The key is to select one of the pure boxing modes ("Rocky" or "Jabs") and try to raise your "Avg Hit Speed" as high as possible (which also has the biggest impact on your scores).

I started out with average hit speeds of around 6 kmh, but recently I've gotten up to around 12 kmh.

In the X-FITNESS COMMUNITY Facebook group, I've seen people post screenshots with average speeds of 15 to 20 kmh. 😲

Have fun! 😅👍

developer_response.body: Thank you for the awesome and detailed feedback! 💪 It’s great to hear how far you’ve come! 🥊 We love seeing players push their limits. Keep smashing those scores!

reviews.review_description: I love fitness games and have spent a small fortune on various games of this type. I can say this is one is a firm favourite from the off. Great music, and a suite of tweaks to increase difficulty by small increments. So many games leap too far when moving up from say medium to expert. I've had Beat Sabre for three years and still can't do anything on the expert setting! Very generous of the developer to add a custom songs option. This is a real bonus. And the conversion process  works well on songs that are fast and slow. Lovely landscapes and nice movement pattern. Really like this and can heartily recommend it as a fitness app for the Quest. I suffer from motion sickness really easily, but this app doesn't generate a moment of queasiness.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing an enthusiastic review! 😊 We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description: Great UI and song choices. TBH many of the fitness apps look similar, but what won me over with X-fitness is that you can upload your own mp3 songs and it does an excellent job at generating a movement track that matches the rhythm of the music. I'm aching from my workouts!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome feedback! We’re thrilled that you love the custom music feature. We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_title: Starting to get fit enough to play
reviews.review_description: I purchased this after Beat Saber  and I hurt some of my muscles I forgot I had in my body but in a good way. I am addicted. Seriously. Worth it.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! 💪 We’re glad you’re enjoying the challenge! Feel free to join our community to connect with other players and share your journey! 🎮😊

reviews.review_title: Cannot share music with second user!
reviews.review_description:
I tried contacting the developer 5 days ago, but have not heard back yet, so am trying this route.

I own 2 Meta Quest 3 headsets.  I am the primary user, my husband is the secondary user, and I share purchased apps with him.  I can successfully load MP3s into the download folder using a google drive.  I can successfully play these MP3s within X-Fitness.

The issue is that my husband is unable to.  He can use the same method and load them into the download folder.  However, he cannot see them in X-Fitness and gets the error message “Access Denied.  Grant App permissions”.

How do we resolve this issue.  We bought this app for an upcoming cruise because it does not require the internet.  We leave in a week, and am hoping you can come up with a solution by then, otherwise we may ask for a refund.

Thanks, Debbi

I have tons of these types of games, I enjoy most of them really, but love this one. I'm 53, work long days and when I don't feel like moving, this gets me going.
The songs that come with it are okay, but I love the customization of picking my own songs and variety of movements. The app does a good job in my opinion of creating and syncing the movements with the song. I have tons of MP3s as it was, so this is a great way to use those. I would say when you pick songs to  import, pick ones with a decent BPM or it will be boring. I stick with about 120 to 140, any higher and I can't keep up...yet!

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing! Glad you're enjoying it! Also, we’d love to invite you to join our community to connect with other players and stay up-to-date on future improvements! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description: Ok, changing my early bad review to a good one now. I finally managed to play my own mp3s and create playlists and workouts to my liking. Loooving it now! Thank you!

developer_response.body: Thank you for giving it another shot and updating your review! 🙌 We're so glad to hear you're loving it now and enjoying your personalized playlists and workouts. 🎶💪 If you haven’t already, feel free to join our community to share your experiences and connect with other players! 😊✨

reviews.review_description:
I love the transparency, how this app has both demo and trial options, so you really know what you're buying, and also that it's not one of those "hidden" subscription style apps. 
The musics are cool, good verity, good energy. It was never so much fun (and tiring, if you want it to be) to exercise at home. :)
Thanks and kudos to the developers S2

developer_response.body: Great to hear you're enjoying it! We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_title: Bought in a bundle with Racket Pinball
reviews.review_description: I like this but I am hoping the maker of this can let me know if I can slow down the speed on it. I am 74 and need for it to be slower. I looked in settings but did not find anything.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your message and for supporting us through the bundle! 😊 We're thrilled you're enjoying the game. Try playing on Easy mode and using MP3s with a lower BPM for a slower pace.  🎵💡

If you need help setting it up or have more questions, feel free to join our community—we’re always here to help and would love to have you with us! 💬🎮

reviews.review_description:
There are many games in this genre. Beat Saber kicked it off and it's okay. Synth Riders has a better sound track and feels more like dancing than waving your arms around. X-Fitness probably has the worst name, but it is by far the best of these type of  games.

While both Beat Saber and Synth Riders can be loaded with custom songs, they have to be programmed by someone. With X-Fitness, it really is as simple as loading an MP3. It creates a really well synchronized level in a second or two. 

Also, X-Fitness has a nice variety of actions to spice things up compared to the other games. I also like the environments here a bit better. There's quite a bit of customizability in the levels/actions. Lastly, the price is right. Love it.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback and kind words! We're so glad you enjoy the game. Keep having fun and enjoying your workouts! 😊 We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description:
So I have tried more than a few fitness apps and found many of them lacking in terms of graphics, customization, and play-ability. More importantly, most of them failed to keep me motivated to actually see any tangible results. However, X-Fitness is freaking spot on! Tons of different workout options, great response, and the "dealmaker," the ability to add your own mp3s to work out to.

 I was skeptical after using other apps that made the claim that you can add your own tunes and have a customized workout, only to find out that all they did was put your music on in the background and the workout never matched up. X-Fitness can actually back up that claim. Was it a bit off on some passages, yeah; but not by much at all, especially considering what I loaded into it. I gave it a complicated 12 minute long song (Canary Yellow-Deafheaven) and it was on point 95% of the time. I won't lie, my arms are sore AF right now, but that's not a bad thing! I was starting to feel the burn and when I looked down at the timer and saw I still had 6 minutes left in the song, I said "bring it on!!" I can't wait to cook up some killer workout playlists for this. Excellent game and an excellent job by the developers in finally getting a VR fitness game right! Plus the price is right in that sweet spot, best 20 bucks I've spent all year! I cannot recommend X-Fitness enough!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the incredible review! 💪 We're thrilled X-Fitness hit the mark for you! 🔥 Feel free to join our community to connect with others, and stay in the loop! 🎶🙌

reviews.review_title: THIS is THE VR workout game now.
reviews.review_description:
Seriously, adding your own custom music makes this the best workout game by far.
 
On top of that, there are many options to customize and fine tune the type of workout you want for each song. 

Got this on sale, but the personal mp3 integration makes this more than worth the purchase at full price.  

Well done.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! We're glad you're loving the custom music feature and the flexibility to tailor your workouts. We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description:
I have several fitness apps, but this is the
 one that I enjoy the most and play every day.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing a review! 😊 We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description:
It gets a 3 because it's deliberately annoying. Add an option so that the app stops disrupting the rhythm and switching the side you have to punch randomly.  No, this behaviour is not useful for fitness purposes, only for games. For fitness it frustrates you, breaks your rhythm and prevents you from having a good workout and keeping your heart rate up. 

Also, longer workouts are desperately needed, the average workout length is less than 2 min, another delivery annoyingly feature that forces you to break the flow of a workout. 

I would be very surprised if the designers of this actually ever used the app

As a 55 yr old male cat, I can say "wow". Got a few rhythm games that will get the pulse running,  but this....ya don't really know your heart is pumping til afterwards,  because your so immersed,  and digging the soundtrack...finish the round, and those of us who wanna do cardio at the gym or weather sucks,or just plain lazy...try this app/game, and look in the mirror and enjoy the smile on your face. Almost like Neo getting that first shot of jiu-jitsu.   "Want some more?"..."Hell yea"

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing such an enthusiastic review! It's awesome to know it brings such a big smile to your face. 😊 We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description: I installed the trial, and within the first minute it was an instant buy for me. I highly recommend it, install the trial, if that doesn't sell you on it,  nothing will.  The loading your own mp3s and the auto-configuring of them is an amazing touch on a gorgeously done fitness app.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We’re so glad the app made such a great first impression. Don’t forget to join our community to share your experience and connect with other fitness fans – we’d love to have you with us! 💪

reviews.review_description: I have several boxing apps and this is my favorite one. The rhythm and punch match perfectly

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! 🥊 We're thrilled to be your favorite boxing app and glad the rhythm feels just right! Come join our community to share your experience and connect with other players! 🎶💪

reviews.review_title: Fitness Has Never Been So Fun
reviews.review_description: I downloaded a trial of this app even though I already had a fitness game I thought I loved.  I was looking at the options screen in-game and saw that it’s by the developers of Racket Pinball.  Knowing full well from just that fact that I’d end up buying it, I started my free trial by using one of the songs I keep on my headset.  I was hooked just as quickly as I was on Racket Pinball and I bought the game with most of my free trial left.   The menu has so many options and the backgrounds range from scenic to surreal and heart-pumping.   You can really tailor it to be a fun game, a powerful workout, or, if you’re like me, both at once.   I highly recommend this app to anyone from beginner to expert, especially if you want a custom workout and your own music.  And if you’re sleeping on Racket Pinball… it’s time to wake up.   Phenomenal and fun, just like this.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review! We’re glad you’re enjoying the app.  We also invite you to join our community to share your thoughts, connect with other players, and stay updated on the latest news! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description: Before this app, less mills was my go to app. But i always wished i could listen to music while working out. So i bought x fitness and tried its playlist feature and its exactly what i wanted!!!! I dont know if theyre using AI to create the obstacles using your music but the tempo is near perfect!! Im in between sets right now but i just cant wait to thank the devs for this masterpiece!! <3

reviews.review_title: Feels useless without proper MP3 mode
reviews.review_description: MP3 mode just doesn't work. There are some major issues that makes it "unusable". I'm sure it could be fixed... Please fix it. I'll probably refund

reviews.review_title: New update made the game worse
reviews.review_description:
I've really loved this game and have played it relatively consistently over the last few years and I'd like to continue supporting these devs with their future releases as well. However the recent update made the game near unplayable for me at times...

Whatever adjustment was made for the syncing/speed/delay of the notes has made fast songs completely out of sync to the point that the 200ms adjustment cannot compensate for it. I now have to stand ~10-11 feet from the feet marker when playing songs in the ~180bpm range. This is for custom songs that I've loaded in (the whole reason I play this game over any other VR rhythm games).

The second new issue is the added sound effects for movement. I turn off all sound effects and leave only the vibration as feedback for my hits and misses. However, the new sound effect for movement is not included in the toggle for sound effects when set to 0, therefore there is no way to get rid of it (the sound for failing to dodge is also not toggleable, which has always been the case, and it would be nice if this was toggleable as well.

reviews.review_title: Great but please fix the track repeating
reviews.review_description: Great for fitness, I appreciate the variability of moves. I mostly enjoy the custom songs and adjustable settings for training composition. But please fix your playlist "randomize" function. I can have 30 songs saved in a playlist but it seems to replay the same 8 all over, very often playing the same song multiple times in a row(!) How hard wold be randomization of the tracks in the playlist and have each played once and then start again with new random order? Please fix this, so I can raise my rating.

I’ve tried a bunch of different workout apps and while some may be fancier than this one, none give me a better workout. Plus, I am extremely motivated to try and get my name at the top of the lists and/or at least beat my own scores — and it has a separate section to compare your scores with your friends.
In a lot of ways I would describe this as beat saber with punches - and a WHOLE LOT OF FUN!
I love the possibility to add custom track and compose the training. What could be add is the change of foot position.
Hate it and they won’t give me a refund even though I canceled it immediately. Buyer beware these people will keep your money.

I purchased box VR (before they did the bait and switch move with fit XR) and it’s a pretty good although dated program. X booster is much more futuristic and has the added feature of having the orbs come at you from different direction so you actually have to turn your body. This can result in a better work out. I haven’t tried adding my own MP3s yet because frankly I don’t care about custom music, but I’m very glad that this company is not going down the subscription route. Not a fan of the exploding orbs. I also really wish there were more environments than this boring futuristic one. I’m not a fan of the bright neon colors. I would much rather for example box on the top of a mountain with the orbs coming at me from the sky or something. Or even a more traditional looking gym environment. Anything other than this strange futuristic environment. It’s very artificial and strains the eyes.

developer_response.body:
Hello, Thank you for your opinion.Have fun!
We created an X-BOOSTER community on Facebook. 
https://www.facebook.com/groups/xboostercommunity
If you are not there yet join us if you like.

reviews.review_description:
Finally! I am free of FitXR. It was such a relief to find, not jist a replacement, but a massive improvement on my old vr workout!

Being able to choose your own mp3 tracks is a phenomenaly brilliant feature!

Love it! 

Keep up the good work devs!

Hello, Thank you for your opinion. Great to hear good words. Have fun!

reviews.review_description: Please change the design. Why did Fitxr succeed? Who made this crazy design? The rest is great, but why do you make a dung net with design? Please make it fitxr.
Awesome workout, I am sweating when done. I play this one daily.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
We improved the hit registration system. Update to version 1.37 should appear soon.

reviews.review_description: a month ago I was downloading this app in demo version, I immediately bought it. this application is simple, it includes everything I need, superstars of different levels and the possibility of loading our own MP3s. I am a shadow boxing coach and I sweat every day for 1 hour to my favorite music. maybe some will not find their account but I have everything I need I find this application great. I remain for a small improvement to propose compared to the playlist, I would be good to have global statistics on the playlist at the end of the course, or a summary for each song. I hope there will be plenty of other new levels, to continue like this is too good. since I work in a gym I might have a business to offer you if you can contact me so we can talk about it. thanks again.
I was getting a great work out on the demo. Decided to buy the full version and it is great. Exercise when it is fun is by far the best and this app is great fun.

reviews.review_description:
I have been trying out a bunch of "exercise" VR apps over the last few weeks and this is one of my favorites. 
I love that the targets come from varying directions so that you have to turn your body-most of these types of games they only come from directly in front of you. I was happy to pay because it's a one time purchase, I can't afford to subscribe to many things!
I enjoy most of the music, the tracks are a bit short but the playlist feature solves that problem :)
I often use this one as a warm up before doing some heavier "boxing" in another, similar app.

reviews.review_description:
With this game there’s more than one way to hit a target.  
Using your shield to collect the power dots, using your sabres to slash oncoming targets and then good old fashioned punching targets.  
It’s a bit much to take in from the get go but your strike movements are automatically there just like your punches and a simply trigger control brings up your shields. 
Personally I think this game is trying to be to many things rather than a simple fitness app. 
The concept is good but just needs a little polishing and it would be right up there with the others in this genre.  

Don’t forget to type the code "FRIEND" at checkout to obtain the 25% discount for the purchase of the app. 

https://www.oculus.com/appreferrals/JezzDangles/3464294860346840/?utm_source=2

reviews.review_title: Rock, punk, post punk and post rock !
reviews.review_description: Finally I can sweat to good music !

reviews.review_description:
How do I update the game? I 



love this game so much. The fact that I can import my own music and the program sets up the targets for you is why I only use this app. I wish I could get rid of the egg shaped object and just box. No other distractions. I’ve turned everything off but haven’t figured that part out yet. The only other thing I would love to see are different backgrounds. Keep up the great work guys. Excellent game.

developer_response.body:
Hello, Thank you for your good words.  If you want just a box you should choose ROCKY or JABS. When you play your own music choose  BOX in the type of workout and turn off drones.

reviews.review_description:
Can only agree with all the other 5-star reviews, and hope it will stay non-subscription so as many people as possible can enjoy this game in the future.

Full version allows perfect adjustment of desired intensity level, as well as use of own mp3's up to 15 mins long.

The trailer for the game is really bad though and urgently needs an update 😂
